comments.text: The most up-to-date improvement is due to Tsz Ho Chan (On moments of gaps between consecutive squarefree numbers, 2023), who shows that the moments exist for all exponents\[ 0 \leq \alpha < 3.75 \]One can find the paper here: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.08448

In Chapter 11 of 'Sieve Methods, Exponential Sums, and their Applications in Number Theory', titled 'Moments and Differences of Square-free Numbers', Huxley shows that you can take $\alpha\leq \frac{11}{3}$ and generalises this to $2k-1+\frac{2}{k+1}$ for the analogous question for $k$-free numbers where $k\geq 3$.
